Rajan Sawhney
  • About Me
  • Work Experience
  • Contact
Techie, Foodie, Soccer-crazy, Tinkering with Blockchain

About Me


Currently, I am seeking full-time opportunities in Software Development. I have obtained my Master's Degree in Computer Science from the University of Oregon (June 2017)

As a member of the High Performance Computing Laboratory at UO I have worked as a Graduate Research Fellow on auto-tuning performance for simulation of large-scale scientific experiments.

I am a self-proclaimed sports-junkie and a football (aka soccer) fanatic


When I'm doing nothing, I have been found discovering new music and reading fiction. Currently listening to LCD Soundsytem and reading "We Have No Idea"

As is the nature of the curious mind to obsess over ideas and keep learning new concepts, I have become involved with blockchain. I have been reading, learning and experimenting with a few blockchain based technologies like Ethereum and IPFS to see where this is leading to.

Download Resume (Last Updated: Jan 30, 2018)

Work & Projects

WORK EXPERIENCE


  • Graduate Research Fellow

    (University of Oregon, Current)

    Working on extending and automating Tuning and Analysis Utilities(TAU) for different High Performance Computing (HPC) applications with with Dr. Boyana Norris for the Department of Energy (DOE).


  • Graduate Teaching Fellow

    (University of Oregon)

    Taught students HTML/CSS and JavaScript as part of the CIS 111-Introduction to Web Programming course

    Taught students to program Raspberry PIs using Python as an introduction to IoT as part of the Hands on with the Internet of Things(IoT) course


  • Software Engineer

    (Accenture Service Pvt. Ltd.,Pune, India)

    SAP ABAP Technical Analyst - Performed analysis and code changes in ABAP programming to correct functionality and usability issues related to the system. Successfully resolved over 50 critical system related issues affecting the clientâ€™s business.

PROJECTS


  • Cluster controlled autonomous hauling system

    Developed a distributed system that allows Arduino based Ringo robots to work in communication with a central server. Used Ricart-Agrawala and Supervisor-worker algorithms to develop the system.


  • Virtual Buttons

    Created an android application using Unity and Vuforia to create Virtual Buttons to interact in an augmented-reality setting.


  • Simulation of Random Walk using MPI

    Developed a project using C that simulated a Random Walk over a big graph data set. Used Message Passing Interface (MPI) for process-to-process communication.


  • Monitoring IoT Systems

    Used Python and InitialState visualization, to develop an application to monitor the system activity of a cluster of RaspberryPIs


  • Face recognition using PCA, SVM and SOM

    Developed a MATLAB project to study various approaches used to address the face recognition problem like Principal Component Analysis(PCA), Support Vector Machine(SVM) and Self-Organizing Map(SOM).


  • Emotion Based Music Player

    Developed a music player application in MATLAB that utilizes emotion recognition to play different categories of music, depending on the facial expression displayed by the user. Created and constructed the algorithm to detect the facial expression.


Contact Me

Email: rsawhney@cs.uoregon.edu

Location

Eugene, Oregon, US
